mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.5% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. Lower FEDFUNDS directly compresses the cost of capital for equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% are not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market tightening that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving positive equity returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods of PAYEMS growth > 150k are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and forward returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rising above 550,000 annualized units trigger a demand-led expansion signal. Higher HOUST indicates construction activity, multiplier effects through employment and materials demand, and forward-looking confidence, producing positive 20-day equity returns when this threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following HOUST > 550k crossings are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or trigger fires in fewer than 30% of expected instances based on historical frequency.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 0.5% signal supply-side contraction or demand weakness that reduces corporate profitability expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice earnings.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines > 0.5% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism triggers in fewer than 20% of months with actual INDPRO declines.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 9.5% combined with flat or declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns through earnings compression.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.5% and PAYEMS at 131,692k as of 2014-12
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or post-shock recovery plateau
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when both UNRATE > 9.5% and PAYEMS declines are observed do not show significantly negative mean returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerating above 3% year-over-year combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) signals broad-based demand strength that supports equity valuations and earnings growth, producing positive 60-day S&P 500 returns through the multiplier effect.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS year-over-year growth > 3% and HOUST > 500,000 annualized units
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 342,138 million and HOUST at 532,000 as of 2014-11
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across housing and retail
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when both RSAFS YoY growth > 3% and HOUST > 500k are observed do not show significantly positive mean returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 2.5% year-over-year in a low-rate environment (FEDFUNDS < 1%) signals real rate compression that erodes purchasing power and forces Fed tightening expectations, producing negative 20-day equity returns as discount rates rise.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 214.459 and FEDFUNDS at 0.21% as of 2014-12
regime_hint: monetary policy constraint with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when both CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 1% are observed do not show significantly negative mean returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerating to below 1% year-over-year while unemployment remains elevated (UNRATE > 9%) signals a stalled recovery with limited pricing power and margin compression, producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations reset downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 95.3964 and UNRATE at 9.5% as of 2014-12
regime_hint: stalled recovery with supply-side slack and labor market weakness
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when both INDPRO YoY growth < 1% and UNRATE > 9% are observed do not show significantly negative mean returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a regression model.
